title: compliance — PHP attribute contracts
description: Five attributes discovered at build time by CompileComplianceRegistries. The compiler maps class + method targets into DsarContributorRegistry + ConsentGate + LegalHoldGate + RetentionPolicyResolver so runtime enforcement is a hash-map lookup, never a reflection call.

attributes.name: DsarExportable
attributes.description: Declares that the annotated model class contributes rows to a DSAR export bundle. The DsarOrchestrator scans registered classes during Dsar.state=collecting to build the artefact list for a subject.

params.name: subject
params.description: Column name on the model pointing at the subject id. e.g. `owner_id`, `user_id`, `subject_id`.

params.name: subject_type
params.description: When the subject is polymorphic, the column name for the morph type (paired with `subject`). Null for single-target FKs.

params.name: include
params.description: Column glob to export. `['*']` = all columns. Explicit list = only these columns. Combine with `exclude` when starting from `['*']`.

params.name: exclude
params.description: Columns to redact from the export. Applied after `include`. Sensitive columns like `password_hash`, `remember_token`, `mfa_secret` MUST be excluded.

params.name: format
params.description: Wire format for the artefact. `json` = pretty-printed object array. `ndjson` = newline-delimited JSON for large tables. `csv` = flattened rows for spreadsheet consumers.

params.name: chunk_size
params.description: Rows per chunk when streaming. The assembler concatenates chunks into the final artefact file.

params.name: priority
params.description: Assembly order. Lower priority runs first — useful when downstream artefacts reference upstream ones (e.g. Files export runs after User export so File.owner_id resolves in the same bundle).

attributes.example:
#[DsarExportable(subject: 'owner_id', exclude: ['password_hash', 'mfa_secret', 'remember_token'], format: 'json')]
final class User extends Model { /* ... */ }

attributes.name: DsarErasable
attributes.description: Declares that the annotated model class supports DSAR erasure. Every DsarExportable class should ALSO be DsarErasable unless a compliance-supported retention prevents deletion (e.g. tax receipts under 7-year regulatory retention — in which case the class is DsarExportable but NOT DsarErasable, and the DSAR request is partially fulfilled with a rejection note for the retained records).

params.name: subject
params.description: Column name on the model pointing at the subject id.

params.name: subject_type
params.description: Polymorphic morph type column when applicable.

params.name: strategy
params.description: Erasure strategy. `hard_delete` = row + relations removed. `anonymize` = subject-identifying columns hashed to a stable pseudonym (email → sha256, name → 'Erased Subject', profile_photo → null). `soft_delete` = deleted_at set.

params.name: retain_shell
params.description: When `strategy=hard_delete`, keep a stub row (id + tenant_id + created_at) for referential-integrity reconciliation. Set true when relations from other tables must survive (e.g. Order.customer_id needs to stay resolvable after Customer erasure for finance).

params.name: priority
params.description: Erasure order. Higher priority runs LAST so child rows are erased before parents.

params.name: on_legal_hold
params.description: Behaviour when the subject has an active LegalHold. `refuse` = the entire DSAR is rejected. `skip` = this contributor is skipped, DSAR proceeds without it, rejection note added. `flag` = artefact created with `held=true`, review-required.

attributes.example:
#[DsarErasable(subject: 'owner_id', strategy: 'anonymize', on_legal_hold: 'skip', priority: 200)]
final class Comment extends Model { /* ... */ }

attributes.name: ConsentRequired
attributes.description: Marks an action / class / job as gated on a specific consent category. Enforced by the ConsentGate facade at method entry OR by the compliance.consent.gate HTTP middleware on routes. Requests / jobs targeting a subject without the required consent get a `ConsentRequiredException` (mapped to HTTP 451 Unavailable For Legal Reasons at the API boundary).

params.name: category
params.description: Consent category key. Must match a row in `consent_categories`.

params.name: subject
params.description: Argument name (for methods) or property name (for jobs) pointing at the subject id. Defaults to `recipient`.

params.name: fail_action
params.description: Behaviour on missing consent. `throw` = ConsentRequiredException. `skip` = method returns early (job dispatches null). `log` = proceeds but writes a warning.

attributes.example:
#[ConsentRequired(category: 'marketing', subject: 'recipient')]
final class SendMarketingEmailJob { public function handle(User $recipient): void { /* ... */ } }

attributes.name: RetentionPolicy
attributes.description: Declares an INLINE retention policy for the annotated model. Overrides the module's own `retention.json` when the retention runner encounters conflicting values (attribute wins for the annotated class only; retention.json wins for every other class in the same module).

params.name: hot_days
params.description: Days a non-soft-deleted row stays in the hot table. Null = indefinite.

params.name: warm_days
params.description: Days a soft-deleted row stays before hard-purge. Null = inherit runner default (30 days).

params.name: cold_days
params.description: Days an archived_at-flagged row stays before hard-purge. Null = inherit module retention.json default.

params.name: on_purge
params.description: Terminal action. `archive_cold` = move to cold-storage table (long-tail regulatory).
params.compliance_note: Regulated data classes (financial receipts under tax law, healthcare records under HIPAA) MUST use `archive_cold` not `hard_delete`.

params.name: regulatory_minimum_days
params.description: Non-negotiable lower bound. The retention runner refuses to purge rows younger than this even when the tenant admin has configured a shorter window. Use for tax retention (2555 days = 7 years) + healthcare retention where applicable.

attributes.example:
#[RetentionPolicy(hot_days: null, cold_days: 2555, on_purge: 'archive_cold', regulatory_minimum_days: 2555)]
final class TaxReceipt extends Model { /* ... */ }

attributes.name: LegalHoldable
attributes.description: Declares that the annotated model class supports legal hold. LegalHoldGate scans registered classes at boot; runtime calls check the LegalHold table for freezes on this model's rows before any purge / erase / hard-delete action.

params.name: subject
params.description: Column pointing at the subject id (nullable when the model participates in tenant / case / class holds but has no per-row subject binding).

params.name: scopes_supported
params.description: Which hold scopes apply to this model. Some models (e.g. platform-level rows) only support `class` scope; tenant-scoped models support all four.

params.name: on_soft_delete
params.description: Behaviour on soft-delete while held. `allow` (default) = user can soft-delete, restore later. `refuse` = LegalHoldActiveException. Hard-delete always refused on held rows.

attributes.example:
#[LegalHoldable(subject: 'owner_id', scopes_supported: ['subject', 'tenant', 'case'])]
final class Message extends Model { /* ... */ }

output_registries: DsarContributorRegistry — map of subject-column → list of contributor class names
ConsentGateRegistry — map of category-key → list of gated actions
LegalHoldGate registry — map of model class → supported hold scopes
RetentionPolicyResolver registry — map of model class → inline policy attribute (falls back to retention.json)
discovery.cache: Redis, keyed on `compliance:registries:<sha256-of-attribute-scan>`. Invalidated on `artisan compliance:describe --refresh` or class-map change.
